compiled_state.title: As a maintainer, the brainstorm UX probe loop has an end-to-end dogfooding regression test
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a new regression test that invokes the brainstorm Phase 1 flow end-to-end on a representative UI feature seed ("add a benefits-status dashboard for claimants"), stubs the haiku classifier (via the mockable interface defined in S2) to return "ui", and asserts that (a) the Understanding Summary contains a Surface bullet, (b) all three UX probes are asked during Phase 1, and (c) the resulting ticket description contains the UX probe answers in a structured location (inline in description or a `## UX Probes` section).

**Why**: SC8 is the authoritative integration signal for this epic — component-level tests on SC1–SC7 are necessary but not sufficient for epic closure. The dogfooding test validates the full loop end-to-end using the same brainstorm a practitioner would invoke.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - Create a new test file covering SC8. Place it in `tests/skills/brainstorm/` if that subdirectory is introduced (per the epic's SC8 wording); otherwise follow the existing flat convention and place it at `tests/skills/test-brainstorm-ux-probe-loop.sh`. Document the chosen path in the test file's header.
  - Make the file executable (`chmod +x`).
  - UI feature seed text: "add a benefits-status dashboard for claimants".
  - Stub the haiku classifier via the mockable interface defined in S2 (env var or script seam). The test must not invoke a live classifier.
  - Craft the seed so it does NOT pre-answer any of the three UX probe topics (interaction criticality, non-happy-path state coverage, flow entry/exit); otherwise S4's anti-redundancy directive will suppress a probe and SC8's "all 3 probes asked" assertion will fail.
  - Three assertions: (a) Surface bullet present in Understanding Summary, (b) all 3 UX probes asked during Phase 1, (c) probe answers present in the resulting ticket description in a structured location.
  - The test must pass before epic closure (ticket closure for SC8).
- OUT:
  - Changes to brainstorm SKILL.md or any other implementation artifact (those belong in S1–S5).
  - Changes to preplanning-side gate (S8).
  - Documentation updates (S7).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the SC8 regression test file exists at the chosen path (see Scope IN for path decision), is executable (`chmod +x`), and passes.
  ← Satisfies: "a new test in tests/skills/brainstorm/ invokes the Phase 1 flow on a representative UI feature seed"
- When this story is complete, the test stubs the haiku classifier via S2's mockable interface to return "ui" and asserts the Understanding Summary contains a Surface bullet.
  ← Satisfies: "stubs the haiku classifier to return 'ui'; asserts that (a) the Understanding Summary contains a Surface bullet"
- When this story is complete, the test asserts all three UX probes (interaction criticality, non-happy-path state coverage, flow entry/exit) are asked during Phase 1.
  ← Satisfies: "(b) all three UX probes are asked during Phase 1"
- When this story is complete, the test asserts the resulting ticket description contains the UX probe answers in a structured location (inline in description or a `## UX Probes` section).
  ← Satisfies: "(c) the resulting ticket description contains the UX probe answers in a structured location (inline in description or a ## UX Probes section)"
- When this story is complete, the test seed is crafted to not pre-answer any of the three probe topics, so S4's anti-redundancy directive does not suppress any probe.
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Testing] Mock-compatible — stub the classifier dispatch via the S2 interface; do not invoke a live classifier.
- [Testing] Seed must be crafted to NOT pre-answer any of the 3 probe topics, otherwise S4's anti-redundancy directive suppresses a probe and the "all 3 probes asked" assertion fails. Cross-reference S4's consideration.
- [Path convention] Existing brainstorm tests live flat at `tests/skills/test-brainstorm-*.sh`; SC8's wording suggests `tests/skills/brainstorm/`. Document the chosen path and rationale in the test file header; either location is acceptable as long as the test is discovered by the test harness and passes.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
